objective: Determine the finite exact Ramsey number R(5,5), currently known to lie between 43 and 46, through a compact 43-vertex lower-bound witness or a certificate-carrying finite upper-bound exclusion.
completion_criteria: For a lower-bound improvement, emit a 43-vertex adjacency artifact and two independently implemented exact checkers that find neither a K5 nor an independent 5-set.

For an upper-bound improvement, preserve a deterministic encoding, checked SAT/UNSAT leaf certificates, a checked exhaustive cube cover, checked symmetry coverage, and an independent generator-to-graph correspondence audit.
A candidate remains unaccepted until isolated reproduction, current-literature review, and Charlie's recorded approval.
non_results: A graph that passes only a shared or heuristic checker is not a Ramsey witness.
A local-search score, a partial SAT cube set, or an unchecked solver result is not a bound.
Rediscovering one of the known 656 order-42 graphs is a control result, not frontier progress.
The 656 known order-42 graphs are not a proved complete census.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.

baseline_review.requirement: Repeat when the maintained survey or primary-source status changes materially.

synthesis_summary: All normalization, mapping, degree-counter, Ramsey-ledger, mutation, and small-instance controls passed. The production CNF had 71,421 variables and 1,844,093 clauses. CaDiCaL returned UNKNOWN after 300.226 seconds; peak child RSS was 524,756 KiB. No witness or exclusion was obtained, so 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46 remains unchanged.

established_facts.claim: The maintained Dynamic Survey records 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
established_facts.scope: Current published bounds in the 2026 research audit

established_facts.claim: The full source trail, historical audit, strategy portfolio, and verification architecture are stored in research/ramsey-r55/DOSSIER.md and must be consulted before proposing a new route.
established_facts.scope: Engine operating instruction

established_facts.claim: McKay's public corpus contains 328 (5,5,42) representatives; complements give the 656 known graphs, but completeness is conjectural.
established_facts.scope: Known order-42 control corpus

established_facts.claim: Every one of the known 656 order-42 graphs has been exhaustively shown non-extendible by one vertex.
established_facts.scope: Direct one-vertex extension of the maintained known corpus

established_facts.claim: If an (5,5,43) graph exists, all 43 of its vertex-deleted order-42 graphs are valid and none belongs to the known 656.
established_facts.evidence: Vertex deletion preserves forbidden K5/independent-5 conditions, combined with exhaustive non-extension of the 656
established_facts.scope: Necessary condition for every possible 43-vertex witness

established_facts.claim: The survey reports that any unknown order-42 graph shares no induced 37-vertex subgraph with the known 656; the underlying exhaustive artifact was not located for replay.
established_facts.scope: Reported McKay-Lieby induced-subgraph-distance computation

established_facts.claim: Known order-42 representatives have 423..430 edges, degrees 19..22, mostly trivial automorphism groups, and none is almost regular.
established_facts.scope: Observed properties of the known corpus, not necessary properties of unknown graphs

established_facts.claim: Every vertex of an (5,5,m) graph has degree between m-25 and 24; a 43-vertex candidate therefore has degrees 18..24.
established_facts.scope: All hypothetical (5,5,m) graphs

established_facts.claim: A reproducible near-cyclic K43 construction has 9 monochromatic K5s; a published continuous-time MaxSAT experiment reports a two-conflict K43 whose two bad cliques lie on six vertices.
established_facts.scope: Published K43 optimization controls; neither is a Ramsey witness or global optimum proof

established_facts.claim: Pure circulant colorings cannot produce an order-42 or order-43 (5,5) graph because C(5,5)=41.
established_facts.scope: Exact circulant family only; almost-circulant symmetry-broken repair remains open

established_facts.claim: The current R(5,5)<=46 theorem used edge-extremal (4,5,n) catalogues, exact excess/counting restrictions, pointed-graph gluing, and two independent implementations consuming roughly 30 plus 50 CPU-years.
established_facts.scope: Published upper-bound architecture

established_facts.claim: No public end-to-end CNF/cube/LRAT package or formal theorem was located for the <=46 computation; independent implementations are not equivalent to a proof-carrying replay.
established_facts.evidence: 2026 artifact audit of the paper, author data page, and public repositories
established_facts.scope: Artifact availability, not validity of the published theorem

established_facts.claim: Successful exact Ramsey verification precedents include HOL4 R(4,5)=25, SAT+CAS certificates for R(3,8) and R(3,9), and a Lean/LRAT-Catcher R(4,4)=18 demonstration.
established_facts.scope: Reusable verification architectures; resource demands differ sharply from R55

established_facts.claim: The maintained published range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
established_facts.evidence: 2026 Dynamic Survey and Angeltveit-McKay journal publication
established_facts.scope: Published global R(5,5) bounds as checked on 2026-07-20

established_facts.claim: The custom short-graph6 parsing and complement plumbing agrees with nauty and NetworkX on every unlabeled order-7 graph class.
established_facts.scope: The 1,044 unlabeled simple graph isomorphism classes at order 7

established_facts.claim: The two exact checkers agree on the violation identities and four valid deletions in the provisional K43 transcription.
established_facts.scope: Only input SHA-256 3beebde4f3120337029bb24de1f737f72eab567cbe92c77f79e7206ce4c304e9; publisher equivalence unverified

established_facts.claim: The embedded NetworkX signature path agrees with both exact checker parsers and nauty complementation on every unlabeled order-7 graph class and complement.
established_facts.scope: 1,044 source representatives and 1,044 complements at order 7 only

established_facts.claim: Complete adjacency fingerprints from three parser paths agree on every unlabeled order-7 graph class and complement.
established_facts.scope: 1,044 representatives and 1,044 complements at order 7 only

established_facts.claim: The maintained published range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
established_facts.scope: Published global status checked 2026-07-20

established_facts.claim: The directly retrieved supplied McKay packet has 328 valid source records whose complements yield 656 distinct supplied order-42 Ramsey graphs under the full gate.
established_facts.scope: Exactly the supplied records and derived complements; not completeness

established_facts.claim: The authenticated publisher seed has exactly two all-zero K5s and no all-one K5.
established_facts.scope: Publisher body SHA-256 c2429869f6fa47ab7388134b580b014efae01e6f0e474f5bab2233afb1ef6990

established_facts.claim: Every one of its 903 one-edge flips has burden at least 2.
established_facts.scope: Hamming distance exactly one from the frozen seed

established_facts.claim: The 741 flips avoiding {6,12,36,42} have minimum burden 3.
established_facts.evidence: Complete strict-family ledger and seven full minimizer identities
established_facts.scope: Exactly the recorded avoid-intersection predicate

established_facts.claim: Every one of the 407253 distinct two-edge flips of the authenticated publisher seed has monochromatic-K5 burden at least 2; the deletion-remainder-change subfamily of 403809 pairs has the same minimum.
established_facts.evidence: artifacts/publisher_seed_radius2_report.json and artifacts/publisher_seed_radius2_scores.u16le in the R55 problem workspace
established_facts.scope: Hamming distance exactly two from the frozen 43-vertex seed

established_facts.claim: The only two radius-2 minimizers are edits {(6,12),(9,15)} and {(33,39),(36,42)}, and both edited graphs are the seed relabeled by vertex rotations -27 and +27 modulo 43.
established_facts.evidence: Dual exact minimizer identities in artifacts/publisher_seed_radius2_report.json; nauty canonical labels and explicit NetworkX isomorphism mappings recorded in CHECKPOINT.md
established_facts.scope: The complete radius-2 minimum set of the authenticated seed

established_facts.claim: Freezing the publisher seed's 20 constant cyclic-distance orbits and varying all 43 distance-6 edges yields minimum burden 2 over all 2^43 assignments. Exactly 86 assignments attain it: every rotation of a step-27 interval of 24 ones or 25 ones.
established_facts.evidence: Independent Python/Z3 and custom C DPLL complete model-set agreement in artifacts/distance6_slice_exact_report.json
established_facts.scope: The exact 43-variable distance-6 slice only, not the full 903-edge space

established_facts.claim: For adjacency rows a_u and a_v of any (5,5,n) graph, d_H(a_u,a_v)=d(u)+d(v)-2|N(u) intersect N(v)|. If uv is an edge then the common-neighbor set has size at most 13; if uv is a nonedge then the common-nonneighbor set has size at most 13.
established_facts.evidence: Direct neighborhood argument using R(3,5)=14: an adjacent pair's common neighbors contain neither a K3 nor an independent 5-set; the complement of a nonadjacent pair's common-nonneighbor graph has the same prohibition. R(3,5)=14 is recorded in Dynamic Survey DS1.18, https://www.cs.rit.edu/~spr/ElJC/ejcram18.pdf.
established_facts.scope: All hypothetical and known (5,5,n) graphs; this is a pairwise row-code constraint, not an existence or nonexistence result.

established_facts.claim: A 2026-07-20 primary-source and public-code audit found mechanism-level Ramsey precedents for two parts of the proposed trapping-set route: Molnar et al. use adaptive weights on unsatisfied R(5,5) clauses, and AlphaEvolve's public ramsey_4_13_138.py performs a violation-focused induced-subgraph hill climb and graft after stagnation. No inspected source canonicalizes recurrent R55 failure cores, builds an isomorphism-type atlas, or tests exact core-conditioned templates on held-out trajectories.
established_facts.scope: Public indexed literature and repositories inspected in DOSSIER S16; absence is not proof of historical nonuse.

established_facts.claim: Evolutionary search is established in Ramsey work: Kunkel used a standard population GA and one-point crossover directly on R(5,5), while AlphaEvolve's public ramsey_4_15_158.py uses marginal edge/orbit success frequencies as a gene pool. No GOMEA, linkage tree, mutual-information linkage model, donor-subset optimal mixing, or exact greedy block acceptance was located in the inspected Ramsey sources or nine public AlphaEvolve programs.
established_facts.scope: Public indexed literature and repositories inspected in DOSSIER S16; the unlocated component is learned multi-edge linkage plus optimal mixing, not generic genetic search or gene-pool memory.

established_facts.claim: The adjacency-row Hamming identity is published general graph theory, and LP/subgraph-count machinery has already been applied directly to R(5,5); flag-algebra SDP has been applied to other small Ramsey numbers. No inspected R55 source applies a Schrijver/Terwilliger triple-distance relaxation to adjacency rows while retaining matrix symmetry, zero diagonal, and edge/nonedge pair colors.
established_facts.scope: Public indexed literature inspected in DOSSIER S16; the pair identity and generic LP/SDP framing are not novelty claims.

established_facts.claim: All 20 fixed-background two-orbit slices are burden-zero UNSAT.
established_facts.scope: Distance 6 plus exactly one distance d in {1,...,21} minus {6}, with the other 19 distance orbits frozen to publisher source SHA-256 c2429869f6fa47ab7388134b580b014efae01e6f0e474f5bab2233afb1ef6990.

established_facts.claim: Every specified fixed-background two-orbit slice has exact minimum raw monochromatic-K5 burden two.
established_facts.evidence: Dual per-origin UNSAT encodings, 40 checked DRAT proofs, 40 checked LRAT proofs, and the authenticated publisher burden-two assignment.
established_facts.scope: Exactly the 20 unsymmetrized slices freeing cyclic distance 6 and one other cyclic distance while freezing the remaining 19 orbits.

established_facts.claim: The supplied 328 graphs and their complements all have degree spread exactly 3.
established_facts.scope: Exactly the supplied 656 controls; not a complete classification

established_facts.claim: The retained unsymmetrized q=20 encoding has 71,421 variables and 1,844,052 clauses.
established_facts.evidence: Independent count reconstruction and C Ramsey-ledger audit
established_facts.scope: The declared order-42 degree-{20,21} CNF

established_facts.claim: Every degree-{20,21} order-42 graph is equivalent under complementation and relabeling to one with N(0)={1,...,20}.
established_facts.evidence: Choose a degree-20 vertex; if none exists, complement the 21-regular graph, then relabel the chosen vertex and its neighbors.
established_facts.scope: All simple order-42 graphs whose degrees lie in {20,21}

established_facts.claim: The normalized q=20 formula has 71,421 variables and 1,844,093 clauses and is exactly the retained unsymmetrized body plus 41 units.
established_facts.evidence: Production and cold normalization reports, including identical audit hash 7b5c4d6d3423068e4eef8f8352f55abbf628f6623312d5d46e9595a516f2be39.
established_facts.scope: The fixed-root order-42 degree-{20,21} CNF only.

established_facts.claim: All 1,760 labelled six-vertex graphs in the degree-{2,3} band admit the analogous fixed-root representative; 70 exercise the complementation case.
established_facts.evidence: Exhaustive scan of all 32,768 labelled six-vertex graphs in q20-normalized.audit-a.json.
established_facts.scope: Order six semantic control; the general coverage statement has a separate direct proof.

established_facts.claim: The general fixed-root coverage lemma holds for all degree-{20,21} order-42 graphs.
established_facts.evidence: Choose a degree-20 vertex; if none exists, complement the 21-regular graph, then relabel the root and its neighbors.
established_facts.scope: All simple order-42 graphs with every degree in {20,21}.

strategies.title: Find a genuinely new 42-vertex component
strategies.mechanism: Jointly destroy at least six vertices, exact-repair boundaries, canonicalize every valid result, and score explicit induced-subgraph novelty away from all 656; run an almost-regular constrained branch beside an unrestricted branch.
strategies.hypothesis: A 43 witness, if it exists, is reachable only through an unknown order-42 basin, and the almost-regular conjecture supplies one sharp search slice.
strategies.discriminating_test: Produce a dual-verified order-42 graph outside all 656 or certify a precisely bounded branch empty; rediscovery of known classes fails the epoch.
strategies.blocker: Requires authenticated corpus, canonicalization, novelty metrics, and independent checkers.

strategies.title: Persistent-conflict breakout search
strategies.mechanism: Maintain exact per-edge deltas over affected 5-sets, tabu tenure, aspiration, weighted persistent conflicts, restarts, and explicit conflict-union/hitting-set metrics; A/B test concentration versus dispersion scoring.
strategies.hypothesis: Conflict topology carries move information that raw monochromatic-K5 count discards.
strategies.discriminating_test: Beat basic tabu/annealing under fixed seeds and matched compute on diverse held-out order-42 controls before receiving K43 budget.
strategies.blocker: Needs trusted controls and benchmark harness.

strategies.family: evolved search programs with exact evaluator
strategies.title: Evolve repair mechanisms, not graph bitstrings
strategies.mechanism: Mutate move scoring, restart schedules, destroy-set selection, penalty updates, and seed portfolios across random, algebraic, cyclic, and hybrid families while keeping the exact verifier immutable.
strategies.hypothesis: Executable strategy diversity can discover repair mechanisms missed by one tuned annealer.
strategies.discriminating_test: Matched-compute improvement on time-to-valid-42, diversity across held-out known classes, and reproducible K43 burden across unseen seeds.
strategies.blocker: No trusted benchmark suite or baseline portfolio exists yet.

strategies.family: exact subgraph-count and SDP-inspired inequality discovery
strategies.title: Discover a new structural reduction before scaling upper-bound search
strategies.mechanism: Mine exact (4,5,n) feature tables for integer subgraph-count inequalities, use LP/SDP only to conjecture coefficients, rationalize them, and prove each candidate by explicit counting or a checked pseudo-Boolean certificate.
strategies.hypothesis: A short new inequality can exclude a nonempty family of hypothetical order-45 neighborhood profiles and make certification finite enough to matter.
strategies.discriminating_test: The exact inequality holds on all controls, has a human/checkable derivation, and cuts profiles admitted by the current degree and excess constraints.
strategies.blocker: Feature tables and an exact catalogue-validation pipeline are not installed.

strategies.title: Build a certified small-case and bounded-slice pipeline
strategies.mechanism: Start with R(3,3)=6 using deterministic CNF, checked cube cover, LRAT-Catcher and cake_lpr, then certify one-vertex extensions or bounded K43 repair neighborhoods with explicit symmetry obligations.
strategies.hypothesis: A cold-start replayable pilot can validate the verifier stack and identify which R55 subcases are feasible under the hard-lane resource ceiling.
strategies.discriminating_test: An isolated replay checks theorem encoding, cover, symmetry, every leaf, hashes, and checker independence without trusting the generating solver.
strategies.blocker: LRAT/Lean/cake_lpr toolchain and artifact-retention budget have not been benchmarked locally.

strategies.title: Second authoritative retrieval followed by one non-overlapping full fail-closed order-42 corpus gate
strategies.mechanism: Hash-linked HTTPS provenance gates two exact evaluators, three full-adjacency parser paths, target nauty complementation, structural controls, and canonical labeling.
strategies.hypothesis: A second direct authoritative retrieval reproduces the frozen McKay bytes and the supplied 328 records plus their 328 complements pass every exact control gate.
strategies.discriminating_test: Require immutable raw HTTP headers, body, transfer metadata, and experiment record, then stop on the first provenance, byte, parser, evaluator, complement, histogram, degree, or canonical-label mismatch.
strategies.blocker: Springer Supplementary Data 4 remains unauthenticated, so no publisher-seed deletion novelty or conflict-core repair claim is admissible yet.
strategies.reopen_condition: Retrieve unchanged publisher bytes with raw provenance and obtain exact agreement under both graph checkers.
strategies.reopen_evidence: The prior McKay-corpus blocker is satisfied by .proof-experiments/20260720-221528-34af71 and artifacts/authenticated_corpus_report.json.

strategies.family: exact destroy-and-repair around a published near-solution
strategies.title: Authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 and exhaust every one-edge flip of its published two-conflict K43 matrix.
strategies.mechanism: Hash-gate two direct publisher retrievals, independently parse the raw matrix in Python and C, and compare complete monochromatic-K5 identity ledgers for all 903 one-edge flips.
strategies.hypothesis: No single-edge flip of the authenticated publisher seed is a Ramsey witness, and every flip avoiding all four shared conflict vertices has at least three monochromatic K5s.
strategies.discriminating_test: Exhaust the lexicographically ordered 903 edges under two materially different exact encodings and compare every violation identity.
strategies.blocker: No exact result exists beyond Hamming radius 1. Radius 2 needs a retained complete pair-score ledger and independent identity checks; larger UNSAT neighborhoods would require proof-carrying encodings.
strategies.reopen_condition: The previous source blocker is closed. Reopen radius 1 only upon a demonstrated provenance or checker defect.
strategies.reopen_evidence: Two byte-identical direct Springer retrievals and the dual-checked report close the publisher-seed authentication blocker.

strategies.mechanism: If radius 2 contains no zero, encode a precisely bounded larger repair family with cardinality constraints and retain checked UNSAT proofs or independently checked SAT models.
strategies.hypothesis: A coordinated edit set beyond radius 2 may escape the local basin while remaining small enough for proof logging.
strategies.discriminating_test: First certify a toy bounded ball and measure proof storage before encoding any production slice.
strategies.rationale: Explicit enumeration scales poorly beyond radius 2, while a proof-carrying bounded encoding preserves exact scope.
strategies.title: If radius 2 contains no zero, encode a precisely bounded larger repair family with cardinality constraints and retain checked UNSAT proofs or independently checked SAT models.

strategies.family: decoder trapping-set analysis transferred from LDPC error-floor research
strategies.title: Canonicalize recurrent Ramsey failure cores before designing escape moves
strategies.mechanism: Treat the 903 edge bits as variable nodes and monochromatic or one-flip-from-monochromatic 5-sets as active check nodes. From deterministic failed K43 trajectories, retain the edges that oscillate or remain in active checks over the final window, build a colored bipartite core, canonicalize it under vertex relabeling, and cluster recurrent core types. For each frequent type, solve a small exact core-plus-boundary MaxSAT neighborhood to obtain an escape template; apply templates only as move proposals and re-evaluate the complete graph exactly.
strategies.hypothesis: A small number of canonical active-core types explain a disproportionate share of low-energy search stalls, and exact core-conditioned escape templates transfer to held-out trajectories better than generic persistent-conflict weighting.
strategies.discriminating_test: Run a fixed-seed trace corpus on damaged held-out order-42 controls and low-burden K43 starts. Continue only if canonical cores recur above label-shuffled controls and templates learned on one split improve paired held-out time-to-escape or best exact burden under the same evaluator-call budget; otherwise discard the analogy.
strategies.blocker: Needs trace capture, colored-core canonicalization, and a matched exact-delta baseline; the authenticated order-42 controls and publisher K43 seed are now available.
strategies.prior_art_status: Partial precedent; no located full R55 application as of the 2026-07-20 public-source audit.
strategies.closest_prior_work: Molnar et al. adaptively weight unsatisfied R55 clauses; Piwakowski uses Ramsey tabu memory; AlphaEvolve ramsey_4_13_138.py selects high-violation vertices, hill-climbs their induced subgraph, and grafts it back after stagnation.
strategies.novelty_boundary: Do not claim violation-local repair as new. Test only whether isomorphism-canonical recurrent-core types plus exact core-boundary templates transfer on held-out runs beyond persistent weighting and AlphaEvolve-style subgraph graft baselines.

strategies.family: linkage-tree gene-pool optimal mixing for gray-box Ramsey MaxSAT
strategies.title: Learn and transfer interacting edge blocks instead of crossing whole graph bitstrings
strategies.mechanism: Maintain a diverse population of exact low-burden colorings. Rebuild a linkage tree each generation from elite-population mutual information, active-K5 co-occurrence, or a hybrid of both. Traverse its edge subsets from small to large, copy one subset from a donor, and accept only moves that pass the exact incremental burden evaluator and the archive's diversity rule. Compare learned, gray-box, hybrid, univariate, and uniform-crossover masks rather than assuming the visible K5 factorization is the best linkage model.
strategies.hypothesis: Dynamic linkage learning identifies epistatic edge blocks whose coordinated transfer preserves useful partial structure and escapes single-edge stalls more reliably than uniform crossover or univariate mixing.
strategies.discriminating_test: Split complement pairs and isomorphism families before constructing 6-to-12-edge-damaged order-42 controls. Under identical evaluator calls and seeds, require learned or hybrid masks to improve held-out valid-42 recovery and median exact burden over univariate mixing, uniform crossover, and exact-delta tabu before spending any K43 budget.
strategies.blocker: Needs an exact incremental evaluator, population-diversity guard, leakage-safe held-out controls, and a small linkage-tree implementation.
strategies.prior_art_status: Adjacent evolutionary precedent; no located Ramsey GOMEA/linkage-tree optimal-mixing application as of the 2026-07-20 public-source audit.
strategies.closest_prior_work: Kunkel applies point mutation and standard one-point crossover directly to R55. AlphaEvolve ramsey_4_15_158.py retains marginal edge and distance-orbit success frequencies as a gene pool. GOMEA/LTGA is established on generic MAX-SAT.
strategies.novelty_boundary: Population search, crossover, and marginal gene-pool memory are already applied. Credit only learned multi-edge linkage, donor-subset traversal, and exact optimal-mixing acceptance if they beat those named baselines.

strategies.family: coding-theory distance distributions and Terwilliger moment bounds
strategies.title: Use adjacency rows as a constrained binary code to search for exact profile exclusions
strategies.mechanism: Represent the n adjacency rows as binary words with weights equal to degrees and pairwise distances d(u)+d(v)-2c(u,v). Add the proved edge/common-neighbor and nonedge/common-nonneighbor bounds from R(3,5)=14, then test a hierarchy: exact rational pair-distance LP first, Schrijver-style triple-intersection positive-semidefinite moments second, and colored edge/nonedge moments only if the relaxed stages cut a profile. Known order-42 graphs are mandatory positive controls; every numerical dual must be rationalized into an exact inequality or checked pseudo-Boolean certificate.
strategies.hypothesis: The adjacency-row pair and triple moment cone excludes at least one degree/common-neighbor profile still admitted by scalar degree and excess constraints, supplying a new structural reduction for an upper-bound slice.
strategies.discriminating_test: Generate exact row weight/intersection distributions for all authenticated controls and enumerate the current hypothetical profiles. Park the route immediately if the rational pair-moment relaxation cuts no new profile; escalate to SDP only after a nonempty exact cut, and reject any cut that fails a known control or cannot be rationalized.
strategies.blocker: The off-the-shelf code bound does not encode adjacency symmetry or the edge/nonedge relation; the first relaxed LP must show information gain before a custom colored moment system is justified.
strategies.prior_art_status: Most precedent-adjacent; no located R55 adjacency-row Terwilliger formulation as of the 2026-07-20 public-source audit.
strategies.closest_prior_work: The adjacency-row Hamming identity is Theorem 2.1 of Ali-Suwilo-Mardiningsih (2019). R55 upper bounds already use integer/linear programming and degree, neighborhood, triangle, and induced-subgraph counts. Flag-algebra SDP has proved other small Ramsey numbers; Schrijver supplies the coding SDP.
strategies.novelty_boundary: Neither the row identity nor generic LP/SDP is new. Treat this as a cheap filter unless a colored row-moment inequality cuts a profile missed by the established R55 subgraph LP and is rationalized exactly.

strategies.title: Proof-carrying raw-origin burden-at-most-one exclusion for all 20 fixed-background two-orbit cyclic slices
strategies.mechanism: Assign one relaxation variable to every raw five-set/color origin, impose independently derived prefix and suffix sequential at-most-one constraints, solve both encodings, and verify DRAT, LRAT, specialization, and full-graph semantics.
strategies.hypothesis: At least one of the 20 fixed-background two-orbit slices has raw monochromatic-K5 burden at most one.
strategies.discriminating_test: For each second distance, solve two unsymmetrized per-origin burden-at-most-one CNFs; accept SAT only after two exact full-graph scans, and accept UNSAT only after DRAT and LRAT verification.
strategies.blocker: The classified family is too narrow to affect R(5,5); releasing a third orbit has no current structural discriminator or credible bounded certificate cost.
strategies.reopen_condition: Reopen this strategy only after a concrete defect in its retained provenance, multiplicity, encoding, mapping, proof, or coverage evidence.
strategies.reopen_evidence: The former burden-one blocker was satisfied by per-origin relaxation, dual AMO encodings, proof replay, and adversarial semantic controls.

strategies.title: Unsymmetrized proof-logging SAT pilot for the almost-regular order-42 q=20 degree band
strategies.mechanism: Encode every order-42 Ramsey constraint and require each degree to be 20 or 21; validate the encoding on exhaustive small controls, then run bounded proof-logging SAT.
strategies.hypothesis: There exists a Ramsey(5,5,42) graph in which every vertex has degree 20 or 21.
strategies.discriminating_test: Run the audited 1,844,052-clause CNF through CaDiCaL for 300 seconds and accept only a dual-checked model or a DRAT/LRAT-checked refutation.
strategies.blocker: The unsymmetrized production solver returned UNKNOWN; there is neither a decoded model nor a completed proof.
strategies.reopen_condition: Change the exact search configuration using the proved fixed-neighborhood normalization, a materially different encoding or solver, or justified additional resources.

strategies.title: Symmetry-normalized proof-logging SAT pilot for the order-42 degree-{20,21} q=20 branch
strategies.mechanism: Represent every complement/relabel orbit by fixing N(0)={1,...,20}, independently audit the resulting 41 units, and run one bounded proof-logging SAT experiment.
strategies.hypothesis: The fixed-root representative makes the q=20 almost-regular branch decidable by CaDiCaL 1.7.3 within 300 seconds and 1 GiB.
strategies.discriminating_test: Require the normalized formula to equal the retained unsymmetrized body plus exactly 41 audited units, then accept only a dual-checked SAT model, DRAT/LRAT-checked UNSAT proof, or explicit timeout.
strategies.blocker: The sound normalized q=20 formula remains unresolved under the available monolithic CaDiCaL proof-logging budget.
strategies.reopen_condition: A checked complete cube cover, materially different encoding or proof-logging solver, justified larger resources, or a demonstrated normalization-packet defect.
strategies.reopen_evidence: The prior unsymmetrized-run condition was satisfied by the independently audited 41-unit fixed-root normalization; that normalized run has now also timed out.

strategies.mechanism: Partition the audited normalized formula with a tiny explicit prefix tree and retain a checked cover plus per-leaf proof or model status.
strategies.hypothesis: The normalized branch contains leaf-level cost heterogeneity that a monolithic CaDiCaL run obscures.
strategies.discriminating_test: A 16-leaf short pilot must resolve at least one leaf or materially improve projected proof throughput while its cover passes independent checking.
strategies.rationale: It changes the search architecture and creates resumable, independently scoped evidence without broadening the mathematical domain.
strategies.title: Partition the audited normalized formula with a tiny explicit prefix tree and retain a checked cover plus per-leaf proof or model status.

open_leads.description: Sweep the 20 exact two-orbit domain-wall slices obtained by releasing cyclic distance 6 with one additional distance orbit.
open_leads.rationale: Radius 2 contains only translations of the one-dimensional defect, while the full one-orbit family is exactly closed at burden 2. Any structured escape must couple the defect to at least one more orbit.
open_leads.next_experiment: For each second distance, derive the 86-variable K5 CNF, quotient the common rotation action, ask first for burden 0, dual-check every SAT model, and retain proof logs before treating any UNSAT slice as certified. Rank nonzero slices by exact minimum and factor-graph width.

open_leads.description: Authenticate the 656 order-42 control corpus and reproduce the 13- and 9-conflict K43 constructions.
open_leads.rationale: Every search claim depends on trusted inputs, independent evaluators, and canonical IDs.
open_leads.next_experiment: Mirror and hash r55_42some.g6; write transparent combination and independent bitset checkers; reproduce edge, degree, complement, and violation identities.

open_leads.description: Test the almost-regular order-42 conjectural slice without treating it as a universal property.
open_leads.rationale: None of the 656 is almost regular, so any solution in the slice is automatically a new component and addresses a named expert conjecture.
open_leads.next_experiment: Encode degree spread <=1 at n=42, benchmark SAT/MaxSAT with multiple symmetry regimes, and require proof logs for bounded exclusions.

open_leads.description: Reproduce or obtain the reported induced-subgraph-distance-six exclusion around the 656.
open_leads.rationale: It would prevent wasted search near known basins, but should not become a hard prune from a survey statement alone.
open_leads.next_experiment: Ask for or locate the McKay-Lieby generation code, coverage manifest, and outputs; otherwise formulate the exact distance predicate and reproduce the smallest bounded cases.

open_leads.description: Build a neutral-network atlas of the 656 under edge flips, switches, and short relabeled Hamming moves.
open_leads.rationale: Landscape position may predict extension quality and reveal productive boundary states without assuming the corpus is complete.
open_leads.next_experiment: Canonicalize the corpus, compute neutral adjacency and best one-vertex MaxSAT extension scores, then test predictors on held-out isomorphism classes.

open_leads.description: Implement a proof-carrying toy Ramsey pipeline before any frontier-wide nonexistence claim.
open_leads.rationale: Encoding, cube coverage, symmetry soundness, proof checking, and artifact replay are distinct obligations.
open_leads.next_experiment: Prove R(3,3)=6 with deterministic CNF, a checked cover, LRAT-Catcher, cake_lpr, immutable hashes, and a cold-start replay.

open_leads.description: Mine edge-extremal (4,5,n) catalogues for exact count inequalities that strengthen the order-45 profile restrictions.
open_leads.rationale: The <=46 authors judge direct scaling excessive; a new theorem-level reduction is the plausible upper-bound lever.
open_leads.next_experiment: Fetch and verify the public r45extreme data, generate exact feature tables, learn candidate coefficients, rationalize them, and falsify on every control before attempting proof.

open_leads.description: Authenticate the 328 McKay source records and their complements.
open_leads.rationale: Every novelty and canonical-comparison claim depends on this immutable control set.
open_leads.next_experiment: Place unchanged bytes at sources/r55_42some.g6 and run the authenticated-corpus command in CHECKPOINT.md.

open_leads.description: Enter the unknown-order-42 basin only after controls pass.
open_leads.rationale: Every deletion of a hypothetical K43 witness must lie outside the historically supplied 656.
open_leads.next_experiment: Canonicalize authenticated seed deletions against the 656, then execute a bounded nonlocal destroy-and-repair discriminator.

open_leads.description: Authenticate the 328 McKay records and their 328 complements.
open_leads.rationale: Every novelty and canonical-comparison claim depends on these controls.
open_leads.next_experiment: Place unchanged bytes at sources/r55_42some.g6 and run the command in CHECKPOINT.md.

open_leads.description: Authenticate the 328 McKay records and their complements.
open_leads.rationale: Every novelty and canonical-comparison claim depends on these controls.
open_leads.next_experiment: Place unchanged bytes with provenance at sources/r55_42some.g6 and run the exact CHECKPOINT.md command.

open_leads.description: Build an LDPC-style atlas of recurrent active failure cores in low-burden Ramsey search trajectories.
open_leads.rationale: Violation-focused repair is already present in Molnar-style adaptive weights and an AlphaEvolve induced-subgraph graft. This lead earns compute only if canonical recurrent types and exact templates add held-out transfer beyond those baselines.
open_leads.next_experiment: Instrument deterministic damaged-42 and authenticated-seed K43 runs; define active checks as violated and 9:1 five-sets; canonicalize final-window colored bipartite cores; compare recurrence against shuffled controls, persistent clause weighting, and AlphaEvolve-style high-violation induced-subgraph grafts; test exact templates only on held-out runs.

open_leads.description: Benchmark linkage-tree optimal mixing on leakage-safe damaged order-42 controls before using it at 43.
open_leads.rationale: Standard R55 crossover and marginal edge/orbit gene-pool memory already exist. This lead earns compute only if learned multi-edge linkage and optimal mixing improve on both precedents under leakage-safe evaluation.
open_leads.next_experiment: Implement Kunkel-style crossover, AlphaEvolve-style marginal edge/orbit memory, univariate mixing, mutual-information linkage, factor-derived linkage, and hybrid masks; split complement/isomorphism families; compare exact recovery rate and burden under paired seeds and matched evaluator calls.

open_leads.description: Test the cheapest adjacency-row coding-theory relaxation before building a custom SDP.
open_leads.rationale: The row identity and generic LP/SDP are established prior art. A small rational LP is justified only as a cheap information-gain test against the existing R55 subgraph-count LP.
open_leads.next_experiment: Reproduce the published adjacency-row Hamming identity and current R55 degree/subgraph constraints; extract exact distributions from all controls; formulate the pair-distance LP for n=43..45; count only profiles not already excluded by the established LP, and stop before SDP if there are none.

open_leads.description: Decide whether any of the 20 two-orbit slices has burden one.
open_leads.rationale: The publisher assignment has burden two in every slice, so checked burden-at-most-one exclusions would establish exact minimum two and fully close this structured family.
open_leads.next_experiment: Add raw-origin relaxation variables and an independently derived at-most-one encoding, validate it on the one-orbit control, then run all 20 cases with proof logging.

open_leads.description: Test the recognized almost-regular order-42 Ramsey slice with a deterministic degree-constrained SAT encoding.
open_leads.rationale: None of the authenticated supplied 656 graphs is almost regular, so a valid result would be outside that corpus and address a named structural slice.
open_leads.next_experiment: Hash-lock the corpus report, reproduce its degree spreads, validate the degree counter on small known instances, then run a 300-second/1-GiB proof-logging pilot.

open_leads.description: Run the symmetry-normalized q=20 SAT pilot with N(0) fixed to vertices 1 through 20.
open_leads.rationale: The normalization has a short coverage proof and should remove a large vertex/complement symmetry burden.
open_leads.next_experiment: Add exactly 41 unit clauses, independently audit coverage and mapping, then run the same 300-second/1-GiB proof-logging gate.

open_leads.description: Test representative bands q=17,18,19 only after the normalized q=20 cost is known.
open_leads.rationale: These complete the complement-reduced almost-regular slice, but premature execution would ignore the observed symmetry bottleneck.
open_leads.next_experiment: Reuse the audited generator with band-specific normalization only after q=20 redirects the cost model.

open_leads.description: Benchmark a 16-leaf proof-carrying cube cover on the audited normalized q=20 formula.
open_leads.rationale: A complete small cover tests resumability and leaf heterogeneity without repeating the failed monolithic configuration.
open_leads.next_experiment: Branch on four deterministic cross-part edge variables, verify all 16 cubes independently, and run a short proof-logging leaf pilot.

open_leads.description: Test q=17,18,19 only after a q=20 cube, solver, or encoding benchmark shows a material proof-cost improvement.
open_leads.rationale: The normalized q=20 monolith still timed out, so identical runs in additional bands currently have low expected information value.
open_leads.next_experiment: Retain the bands without executing them until the q=20 cost model changes.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Directly append one vertex to any of the known 656 order-42 graphs.
ruled_out.reason: Every member was exhaustively tested and is non-extendible.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The source corpus/hash is shown wrong or a genuinely new order-42 graph is found.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Pure circulant search for an order-42 or order-43 witness.
ruled_out.reason: The computationally established circulant Ramsey number C(5,5) is 41.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Use an almost-circulant seed with symmetry-breaking repair; do not relabel that as pure circulant search.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Blind ordinary annealing, tabu, or bitstring genetic search counted as progress when it only rediscovers known classes.
ruled_out.scope: Unbenchmarked repetitions without novelty or matched-compute improvement
ruled_out.reason: Historical searches produced thousands to tens of millions of outputs yet only the known corpus; naive GA was far worse than modern near-solutions.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A materially different mechanism beats held-out controls and baseline burden under matched compute.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat the 656 as an exhaustive classification.
ruled_out.scope: Any global proof or pruning step
ruled_out.reason: Completeness is strongly supported but not proved.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A replayable exhaustive classification with checked coverage is produced.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Scale the R(5,5)<=46 gluing computation directly to 45 without a new structural reduction.
ruled_out.scope: Production-scale repetition of the same architecture
ruled_out.reason: The authors report 30 plus 50 CPU-years at 46 and judge direct improvement computationally excessive.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A proved new inequality, reduction, or benchmarked order-of-magnitude algorithmic gain changes the cost model.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Accept UNSAT, symmetry pruning, catalogue completeness, or cube coverage without independently checkable artifacts.
ruled_out.reason: These are separate soundness obligations; solver success or implementation agreement does not prove coverage.
ruled_out.evidence: LRAT/SAT+CAS/HOL4 verification precedents audited in research/ramsey-r55/DOSSIER.md
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Proof logs, checked cover, explicit symmetry proof, immutable hashes, and independent replay all pass.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Use structural similarity, a neural score, floating-point optimization, or LLM judgment as the final evaluator.
ruled_out.scope: Witness validity, inequality validity, and proof acceptance
ruled_out.reason: Structural transfer can improve similarity while exact Ramsey validity worsens.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Use such signals only to propose moves or coefficients, followed by exact checking.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat the visible third-party K43 transcription as the authenticated Springer supplementary seed.
ruled_out.reason: Checker agreement proves properties of supplied bytes but not their publisher provenance.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Recover unchanged Springer Supplementary Data 4 and establish an exact transformation or byte correspondence.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Start novel-42 or K43 repair search from the current control packet.
ruled_out.scope: This epoch and any continuation lacking the raw corpus
ruled_out.reason: The required source preflight failed before any corpus validation or canonical comparison.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The authoritative corpus passes the complete dual-check, complement, histogram, degree, and canonical-label gate.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Start order-42 novelty or K43 repair search from the current packet.
ruled_out.scope: Any continuation lacking the authenticated McKay corpus and Springer seed
ruled_out.reason: The source gate failed before every target-instance check.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The authoritative corpus passes the enhanced two-evaluator, three-parser gate.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat arXiv:2508.16699 as establishing R(5,5)=45.
ruled_out.reason: It presents randomized numerical diagnostics and a prime-sequence heuristic, not a witness or exhaustive exclusion.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Supply an independently checked witness or a complete proof certificate.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Retry direct curl acquisition from ANU or Hugging Face in the unchanged command environment.
ruled_out.reason: Both hosts fail DNS resolution before any HTTP response.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A new network/export route or user-supplied raw file with provenance becomes available.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Start order-42 novelty or K43 repair search from the current packet.
ruled_out.scope: Any continuation lacking authenticated McKay and Springer bytes
ruled_out.reason: The source preflight stopped before every target-instance check.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The McKay corpus passes the complete-fingerprint gate and the Springer seed then passes its raw-source gate.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Assert that the captured authoritative response supplied Content-Type: text/plain.
ruled_out.scope: The direct ANU HTTP/2 response captured on 2026-07-20
ruled_out.reason: Both the raw header block and curl metadata show Content-Type absent.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Preserve a later authoritative response separately if it actually supplies the field.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat 656 distinct supplied canonical labels as an exhaustive classification.
ruled_out.reason: The gate contains no exhaustive generator or checked coverage certificate.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Produce a replayable exhaustive enumeration with checked coverage.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Repair the authenticated publisher seed using exactly one edge flip.
ruled_out.reason: The exact global minimum burden remains 2.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Demonstrate a source-byte, parser, or checker defect against the retained controls; larger radii are a different route.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Escape all four known deletion remainders using one edge that avoids their deleted vertices.
ruled_out.reason: The exact minimum burden is 3.
ruled_out.evidence: avoid_all_shared_conflict_vertices family in the retained report
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Use at least two coordinated edits or a materially different seed.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Repair the authenticated publisher seed using exactly two distinct edge flips.
ruled_out.scope: All C(903,2)=407253 pairs, including the 403809-pair deletion-remainder-change slice
ruled_out.reason: The exact minimum burden remains 2; the only two minimizers are isomorphic rotations of the seed.
ruled_out.evidence: artifacts/publisher_seed_radius2_report.json and the complete compact score ledger
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Demonstrate a source, ordering, or checker defect; larger edit sets or released orbit families are different routes.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Find a K43 witness by varying only the 43 cyclic-distance-6 edges of the authenticated seed.
ruled_out.scope: All 2^43 words with the other 20 cyclic-distance orbits frozen
ruled_out.reason: Independent complete enumerators prove minimum burden 2 and classify all 86 optima.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Only after a defect is found in the reduced-clause derivation or either complete model-set check; releasing another orbit is a different family.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Find a K43 Ramsey witness in the authenticated seed's two-released-orbit domain-wall family.
ruled_out.scope: The union of all 20 archived unsymmetrized 86-variable slices.
ruled_out.reason: Every exact burden-zero CNF has independently checked DRAT and LRAT evidence of UNSAT.
ruled_out.evidence: artifacts/two_orbit_slices, artifacts/two_orbit_lrat, and the two retained reports
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Demonstrate a source, clause-derivation, variable-mapping, proof-checker, or 20-distance coverage defect; freeing a third orbit is a different route.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Lower the monochromatic-K5 burden below two in the fixed-background two-released-orbit family.
ruled_out.reason: Both raw-origin burden-at-most-one encodings are proof-checked UNSAT in every slice.
ruled_out.evidence: artifacts/two_orbit_burden_one, artifacts/two_orbit_burden_one_lrat, and the two retained reports.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Demonstrate a source, raw-origin multiplicity, specialization, cardinality, graph-mapping, proof-checker, or distance-coverage defect.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Repeat the identical unsymmetrized q=20 CaDiCaL 1.7.3 run under the same 300-second/1-GiB budget.
ruled_out.scope: This exact formula, solver, options, and resource limit
ruled_out.reason: The solver returned UNKNOWN, so an identical replay has low information value.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Use a sound symmetry normalization, materially different encoding or solver, justified larger budget, or identify an artifact defect.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat the retained partial DRAT stream as an UNSAT certificate.
ruled_out.reason: Fresh drat-trim returned 1 and did not verify it.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Only a completed independently verified proof can establish UNSAT.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Repeat the identical fixed-root q=20 CaDiCaL 1.7.3 run with the same formula, options, 300-second limit, and 1-GiB ceiling.
ruled_out.reason: It returned UNKNOWN, so an identical replay has low information value.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Use a checked cube cover, materially different proof-logging solver or encoding, justified additional resources, or demonstrate an artifact defect.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat the normalized run's interrupted DRAT stream as an UNSAT certificate.
ruled_out.reason: Freshly compiled drat-trim returned 1 and did not verify it.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Obtain a completed proof that passes independent DRAT and LRAT checking.

unresolved_questions: Does releasing distance 6 together with any one additional cyclic-distance orbit permit burden below 2?
Can the reported distance-six exclusion around the 656 be replayed or obtained with a coverage manifest?
Does the almost-regular order-42 slice contain a new Ramsey graph?
Which exact subgraph-count or small-graph SDP ideas can be converted into a human/checkable order-45 restriction?
Can proof logs for useful bounded R55 subcases fit the hard-lane retention and replay budget?

next_session.objective: Determine whether a tiny complete cube cover exposes tractable or heterogeneous leaves in the normalized q=20 branch.
next_session.first_action: Implement a deterministic 16-leaf cover on four declared cross-part edge variables and validate its coverage and certificate path on normalized R(3,3,6).
next_session.stop_condition: Stop on any cover, mapping, or proof mismatch; otherwise stop if no production leaf resolves under the short pilot or projected certificate storage is not materially improved.
